guideline_references: British Society for Haematology — Guidelines on the diagnosis and management of thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura and other thrombotic microangiopathies (2023)
ISTH Guidelines for the diagnosis and treatment of thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura (2020)
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection (central for HMO): the source note is a physically-present clinician who obtained vitals, examined the patient, and arranged emergency plasma exchange. The AI Doctor CANNOT draw blood, run a blood film / coagulation panel / ADAMTS13 assay, examine the patient, or deliver plasma exchange, and is scored on recognising a catastrophic systemic emergency (fever + fluctuating confusion + spontaneous bleeding/purpura + dark urine) and escalating to 000 / tertiary ED — NOT on minting the specific diagnosis or ordering the treatment.

Genuine haematological emergency: a carer can self-report every time-critical feature (sudden confusion/slurred speech, high fever, spontaneous mucosal bleeding, dark urine), so the correct tier is T5 despite the AI's diagnostic ceiling.

Can't-miss overlap: fever + altered mental state + a purpuric rash must be treated empirically as meningococcal sepsis until excluded — the case tests whether the system holds that concern while escalating.
Sex at birth not stated in the source note (gender-neutral pronouns used) — inferred as female and flagged. Age 38 explicit. Spouse is the sole informant.
Source category: HMO (Haematology & Oncology), source case HMO-045.
